title: SSL/TLS certificate validation bypass via ServerCertificateValidationCallback
description: Assigning a callback to [Net.ServicePointManager]::ServerCertificateValidationCallback that unconditionally returns $true disables certificate validation entirely. This is an OWASP A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures vulnerability that exposes all HTTPS traffic to man-in-the-middle attacks. Common workaround pattern in dev scripts that was never reverted before production use.
detection_method: Regex scan for ServicePointManager ServerCertificateValidationCallback assignment
preventionRule: Never assign a custom ServerCertificateValidationCallback. Use proper certificate trust chains or import self-signed certs into the trust store. If a specific cert fingerprint must be accepted, verify the thumbprint explicitly instead of returning $true unconditionally. Mark intentional exceptions with #SIN-EXEMPT: and document the justification.
remedy: Remove or restrict the callback. If testing against a self-signed certificate, use -SkipCertificateCheck (PS 7+) or add a thumbprint validation: { param($s,$c,$ch,$err); $c.Thumbprint -eq '<expected>' }. Never use { $true } in production code.
